title: Stress Dysregulation Cycle
definition: A pathophysiological vicious cycle in which chronic psychological or physiological stress leads to dysregulation of the Hypothalamic-Pituitary-Adrenal (HPA) axis.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A recurring, harmful response pattern triggered by prolonged stress that affects how our body responds to stress and can lead to symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, burnout, and muscle tension.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: A positive feedback loop in which chronic stress leads to dysregulation of the HPA axis, resulting in a hypersensitive stress response and an exaggerated neuroendocrine cascade, causing adrenal exhaustion and cortisol resistance.
difficulty_levels.expert: A critical systems-level pattern characterized by homeostatic failure, where chronic stress perpetuates the dysregulation of the HPA axis, leading to hypersensitivity to minor perturbations, resulting in a disproportionate neuroendocrine cascade and reinforcing the original chronic stress state.

extra_fields.insight: The link between chronic stress, adrenal exhaustion, cortisol resistance, and hypersensitivity to small stressors, forming a self-perpetuating cycle.
